title: Chinese Garlic Green Beans

ingredients.name: green beans (French beans), trimmed and snapped in half

subs.name: asparagus spears or long yard-long beans
subs.notes: yard-long beans (common in South India) work particularly well and are more authentic

subs.name: 3–4 Kashmiri dried chillies
subs.notes: milder but give a deep red colour

subs.name: 1 tbsp salt dissolved in a dash of water
subs.notes: soy sauce adds umami that plain salt cannot fully replicate

steps.text: Pat the trimmed green beans completely dry with a kitchen cloth — moisture in the wok will cause dangerous splattering and prevents blistering.

Heat oil in a wok or kadai over the highest flame until it begins to smoke. Add the green beans in a single layer. Do not toss immediately — let them sear for 1 minute, then toss and cook for another 3–4 minutes, tossing every 30 seconds, until the skins are wrinkled and lightly charred in spots.
Push the beans to the sides. In the centre of the wok, add the dried chillies, garlic and ginger. Stir for 30 seconds until fragrant, then toss everything together.
Add soy sauce and vinegar if using. Toss vigorously for 1 minute until the beans are evenly coated and the sauce has been absorbed. Taste and adjust salt.
Transfer to a serving plate. Scatter sesame seeds over the top if using. Serve as a side with steamed rice and other dishes.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: beans → asparagus spears or long yard-long beans (yard-long beans (common in South India) work particularly well and are more authentic); soy-sauce → 1 tbsp salt dissolved in a dash of water (soy sauce adds umami that plain salt cannot fully replicate); dried-red-chilli → 3–4 Kashmiri dried chillies (milder but give a deep red colour)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: French beans (sold as 'beans' in Indian markets) are perfect for this dish. A wide kadai on the highest gas flame replicates wok heat admirably — just ensure the beans are bone-dry before they hit the oil. Kashmiri or guntur dried red chillies work beautifully. The dish is naturally suited to an Indian kitchen and takes under 15 minutes start to finish.
